You are the Targeted Verifier agent in the repo-local pi-flows pilot executor.
Executor: pi-flows
Flow: hansori-review-feedback-v1
Step: verify
Purpose: Verify the remediation and summarize any remaining actionable issues.
Branch: agent/mindful-engineer/tsk_1776900175295_yo4nn6-add-content-pipeline-and-feeds
Worktree: /home/benjamin/worktrees/mindful-engineer/agent__mindful-engineer__tsk_1776900175295_yo4nn6-add-content-pipeline-and-feeds
Task id: rvw_1776913107118_z7c3zh
Task title: Address review summary for PR #2
Task body: Address the following GitHub review feedback:

#4159148819: ## Code Review

This pull request transitions the site's data layer from hardcoded fixtures to a dynamic content pipeline using Astro Content Collections and MDX. It introduces structured schemas for articles, notes, links, snippets, and pages, and implements automated RSS feed generation. Feedback focuses on optimizing build performance by caching collection data and refining the handling of external link metadata. Additionally, it is recommended to replace manual regex-based HTML stripping and XML escaping with more robust, standard utilities to ensure data integrity and prevent parsing errors.
Verify only what is needed for the review remediation. When checking whether implementation changes are meaningful, inspect both uncommitted and committed diffs (for example git status plus diff against base/head), not only the current working tree. Report actionable blockers clearly and avoid broad, noisy summaries.

content.text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?><rss version="2.0"><channel><title>The Mindful Engineer</title><description>A monastery-first public reading site for articles, notes, links, snippets, and durable working notes about software and the people living inside it.</description><link>https://mindful.engineer/</link><language>en-us</language><managingEditor>mail@mindful.engineer (Benjamin Kitt)</managingEditor><item><title>Speed without presence is just chaos with a deploy pipeline.</title><link>https://mindful.engineer/notes/speed-without-presence</link><guid isPermaLink="true">https://mindful.engineer/notes/speed-without-presence</guid><description>Speed without presence is just chaos with a deploy pipeline.</description><pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 09:14:00 GMT</pubDate></item><item><title>A good changelog entry is the cheapest act of care a fast team can perform.</title><link>https://mindful.engineer/notes/changelog-as-care</link><guid isPermaLink="true">https://mindful.engineer/notes/changelog-as-care</guid><description>A good changelog entry is the cheapest act of care a fast team can perform.</description><pubDate>Tue, 14 Apr 2026 16:02:00 GMT</pubDate></item><item><title>Fast and Present Are Not Opposites</title><link>https://mindful.engineer/articles/fast-and-present</link><guid isPermaLink="true">https://mindful.engineer/articles/fast-and-present</guid><description>Mindful engineering is not slow engineering. It is fast engineering done with both eyes open: to the work and to the people the work lands on.</description><pubDate>Sun, 12 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate></item><item><title>Paired for an hour with the teammate who joined last month. They are not behind.…</title><link>https://mindful.engineer/notes/they-are-arriving</link><guid isPermaLink="true">https://mindful.engineer/notes/they-are-arriving</guid><description>Paired for an hour with the teammate who joined last month. They are not behind. They are arriving.</description><pubDate>Sat, 11 Apr 2026 07:40:00 GMT</pubDate></item><item><title>Worse Is Better, twenty-five years on</title><link>https://mindful.engineer/links/worse-is-better</link><guid isPermaLink="true">https://mindful.engineer/links/worse-is-better</guid><description>Worth revisiting every couple of years. What felt contrarian in 2000 reads like common sense now, which is itself the point.

External URL: https://gabriellipman.net/worse-is-better-twenty-five-years-on</description><pubDate>Fri, 10 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate><mindful:externalUrl xmlns:mindful="https://mindful.engineer/ns">https://gabriellipman.net/worse-is-better-twenty-five-years-on</mindful:externalUrl></item><item><title>FOMO showed up on schedule this morning. Named it, thanked it, kept shipping the…</title><link>https://mindful.engineer/notes/fomo-weather</link><guid isPermaLink="true">https://mindful.engineer/notes/fomo-weather</guid><description>FOMO showed up on schedule this morning. Named it, thanked it, kept shipping the thing I had already chosen.</description><pubDate>Wed, 08 Apr 2026 22:11:00 GMT</pubDate></item><item><title>Retry with jitter, honestly</title><link>https://mindful.engineer/snippets/retry-with-jitter</link><guid isPermaLink="true">https://mindful.engineer/snippets/retry-with-jitter</guid><description>Exponential backoff, full jitter, and no pretend sophistication.

def retry(fn, attempts=5, base=0.25):
    for index in range(attempts):
        try:
            return fn()
        except Retryable:
            if index == attempts - 1:
                raise
            sleep(base * (2 ** index) + random() * base)
</description><pubDate>Tue, 07 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate></item><item><title>Attention Economy Is A Debt</title><link>https://mindful.engineer/links/attention-is-debt</link><guid isPermaLink="true">https://mindful.engineer/links/attention-is-debt</guid><description>The line that stuck with me is that every push notification is a small loan against your future concentration. Once you see it that way, a lot of product decisions get easier to judge.

External URL: https://new.public/attention-economy-is-a-debt</description><pubDate>Sun, 05 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate><mindful:externalUrl xmlns:mindful="https://mindful.engineer/ns">https://new.public/attention-economy-is-a-debt</mindful:externalUrl></item><item><title>The only git alias I have kept</title><link>https://mindful.engineer/snippets/git-graph-alias</link><guid isPermaLink="true">https://mindful.engineer/snippets/git-graph-alias</guid><description>Everything else I tried was more clever than useful.

[alias]
    graph = log --oneline --graph --decorate --all
</description><pubDate>Mon, 30 Mar 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate></item><item><title>FOMO as a Compass, Not a Whip</title><link>https://mindful.engineer/articles/fomo-as-compass</link><guid isPermaLink="true">https://mindful.engineer/articles/fomo-as-compass</guid><description>There is too much to learn. That is the permanent condition of software, not a temporary problem to outrun.</description><pubDate>Sat, 28 Mar 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate></item><item><title>Nobody Wants to Be the Person Left Behind</title><link>https://mindful.engineer/articles/nobody-wants-to-be-left-behind</link><guid isPermaLink="true">https://mindful.engineer/articles/nobody-wants-to-be-left-behind</guid><description>Every fast team generates, as a byproduct, people who feel one version behind. Pretending otherwise is its own kind of carelessness.</description><pubDate>Sat, 14 Mar 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate></item></channel></rss>---NOTES---

External URL: https://new.public/attention-economy-is-a-debt</description><pubDate>Sun, 05 Apr 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate></item></channel></rss>

Verification result: remediation is partially successful, but there is 1 actionable blocker left.

Blocker
- Undeclared runtime dependencies are imported directly.
  - `apps/public-site/src/data/feed.ts` imports:
    - `entities`
    - `fast-xml-parser`
    - `hast-util-from-html`
    - `hast-util-to-text`
  - But `apps/public-site/package.json` only declares `@astrojs/rss` in `dependencies`.
  - `npm ls` shows those packages are currently available only transitively via `@astrojs/mdx` / `@astrojs/rss`, not as direct deps.
  - This makes the remediation fragile: a lockfile refresh or upstream dependency change can break installs/builds even though the code imports them directly.
  - Action: add those packages as direct dependencies in `apps/public-site/package.json`.

What I verified
- Checked both committed changes (`git diff origin/main...HEAD`) and uncommitted remediation (`git diff`).
- Build-performance remediation is meaningful:
  - content collection loading is now cached via `loadedContentPromise`
  - article source lookup is cached via `articleSources` instead of re-calling `getCollection()` in `getArticleContentBySlug`
  - external link title fetching was removed from the uncommitted diff, avoiding network-dependent build behavior
- Feed hardening remediation is meaningful:
  - manual regex HTML stripping was replaced with `hast-util-from-html` + `hast-util-to-text`
  - manual XML escaping was replaced with XML generation via `fast-xml-parser`
- Validation:
  - `npm run build` passes
  - `npm run check` passes with only deprecation hints for `XMLBuilder`
